title: zephyranthes crassibulba
intro: Zephyranthes crassibulba, also known as the Red Butterfly Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, characterized by its showy, bell-shaped flowers and strap-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, derived from its alkaloid compounds such as crassin and zephyranthine. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Zephyranthes crassibulba is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
